---
title: "ModelCreationTemplates"
format: html
editor: visual
---

HighHost Binary Variable Creation:
```{r}
# Example only: replace 0.80 with your chosen percentile
cutoff <- quantile(trainData$host_listings_count, probs = 0.80, na.rm = TRUE)

trainData$HighHost <- ifelse(trainData$host_listings_count > cutoff, "Yes", "No")
trainData$HighHost <- factor(trainData$HighHost, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
```

Required Packages:
```{r}
library(glmnetUtils)
library(randomForest)
library(gbm)
library(caret)
```

Pre Model Fitting Checks:
```{r}
if (!exists("trainData")) {
  stop("trainData does not exist. Please create your training set first.")
}

if (!"HighHost" %in% names(trainData)) {
  stop("trainData must contain a binary response variable named 'HighHost'.")
}

# HighHost should be a 2-level factor for most of the models below
# Recommended coding:
# trainData$HighHost <- factor(trainData$HighHost, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))

if (!is.factor(trainData$HighHost)) {
  stop("trainData$HighHost should be a factor with two levels, e.g. c('No', 'Yes').")
}

if (length(levels(trainData$HighHost)) != 2) {
  stop("trainData$HighHost must have exactly 2 levels.")
}
```

REGULARISED ELASTIC-NET LOGISTIC REGRESSION:
```{r}
elasticNetFile <- "elasticNetModel.RData"

if (file.exists(elasticNetFile)) {
  load(elasticNetFile)
} else {
  set.seed(4026)

  elasticNetModel <- cv.glmnet(
    HighHost ~ .,
    data = trainData,
    family = "binomial",
    alpha = 0.5,       # template value; later you may tune this
    nfolds = 5
  )

  save(elasticNetModel, file = elasticNetFile)
}

# To inspect:
# print(elasticNetModel)
# coef(elasticNetModel, s = "lambda.min")
# plot(elasticNetModel)
```

RANDOM FOREST:
```{r}
if (file.exists(randomForestFile)) {
  load(randomForestFile)
} else {
  set.seed(4026)

  randomForestModel <- randomForest(
    HighHost ~ .,
    data = trainData,
    ntree = 500,
    mtry = floor(sqrt(ncol(trainData) - 1)),
    importance = TRUE
  )

  save(randomForestModel, file = randomForestFile)
}

# To inspect:
# print(randomForestModel)
# importance(randomForestModel)
# varImpPlot(randomForestModel)
```

BOOSTED TREE MODEL:
```{r}
if (file.exists(gbmFile)) {
  load(gbmFile)
} else {
  set.seed(4026)

  # gbm works best with a numeric 0/1 response for Bernoulli
  gbmTrainData <- trainData
  gbmTrainData$HighHostNum <- ifelse(gbmTrainData$HighHost == levels(gbmTrainData$HighHost)[2], 1, 0)

  gbmModel <- gbm(
    formula = HighHostNum ~ . - HighHost,
    data = gbmTrainData,
    distribution = "bernoulli",
    n.trees = 1000,
    interaction.depth = 3,
    shrinkage = 0.01,
    n.minobsinnode = 10,
    cv.folds = 5,
    verbose = FALSE
  )

  save(gbmModel, file = gbmFile)
}

# To inspect:
# print(gbmModel)
# bestTrees <- gbm.perf(gbmModel, method = "cv")
# summary(gbmModel, n.trees = bestTrees)
```

K-NEAREST NEIGHBOURS:
```{r}
knnFile <- "knnModel.RData"

if (file.exists(knnFile)) {
  load(knnFile)
} else {
  set.seed(4026)

  # caret requires syntactically valid factor levels for class probabilities
  # Recommended levels: "No" and "Yes"
  if (!all(levels(trainData$HighHost) %in% c("No", "Yes"))) {
    stop("For caret KNN, code HighHost as factor levels c('No', 'Yes').")
  }

  knnControl <- trainControl(
    method = "cv",
    number = 5,
    classProbs = TRUE,
    summaryFunction = twoClassSummary
  )
knnGrid <- expand.grid(k = seq(3, 25, by = 2))

  knnModel <- train(
    HighHost ~ .,
    data = trainData,
    method = "knn",
    trControl = knnControl,
    tuneGrid = knnGrid,
    metric = "ROC",
    preProcess = c("center", "scale")
  )

  save(knnModel, file = knnFile)
}

# To inspect:
# print(knnModel)
# plot(knnModel)
```

LOADING SAVED MODELS LATER:
```{r}
# load("elasticNetModel.RData")
# load("randomForestModel.RData")
# load("gbmModel.RData")
# load("knnModel.RData")
```
